chapter_title.en: Chapter 32 – Regarding the drinks, as well

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/294)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (2/294)
text: كَتَبْتُ إِلَى أَبِي الْحَسَنِ الْأَوَّلِ ( عليه السلام ) أَسْأَلُهُ عَنْ أَشْرِبَةٍ تَكُونُ قِبَلَنَا السِّكَنْجَبِينِ وَ الْجُلَّابِ وَ رُبِّ التُّوتِ وَ رُبِّ الرُّمَّانِ وَ رُبِّ السَّفَرْجَلِ وَ رُبِّ التُّفَّاحِ إِذَا كَانَ الَّذِي يَبِيعُهَا غَيْرَ عَارِفٍ وَ هِيَ تُبَاعُ فِي أَسْوَاقِنَا فَكَتَبَ جَائِزٌ لَا بَأْسَ بِهَا .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin Yahy, from Hamdan Bin Suleyman, from Ali Bin Al Hassan, from Ja’far Bin Ahmad Al Makfouf who said,

‘I wrote to Abu Al-Hassan<sup>asws</sup> the 1st asking him<sup>asws</sup> about drinking what happens to be facing us, and the ginger drink, and the rose water, and the mulberry jam, and the pomegranate ham, and the quince jam, and the apple jam, when we have bought which is being sold without recognition, and it is being sold in our markets’. So he<sup>asws</sup> wrote: ‘Allowed, there is no problem with these’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Hamdan ibn Sulayman from Ali ibn al-Hassan from Ja‘far ibn Ahmad al-Makfuf who has said the following: “I once wrote to abu al-Hassan, al-Awwal, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, and asked about drinks like oxymel, rose- water, jam of mulberry, pomegranate, quince and apple; if the seller is not knowledgeable when they are sold in our market places. He (the Imam) wrote, ‘It is permissible and it is not harmful. 5 ”
